text.title: AI Amplification Principle and Token-First Rules
text.summary: Core principle that AI amplifies what exists in a design system, prerequisites for effective AI tooling, and token-first rules.

rule.statement: Before connecting any AI tooling to a design system, verify these prerequisites exist: a canonical token format (JSON/W3C Design Tokens spec), consistent component naming across Figma and codebase, and agreement on a single source of truth for tokens.
rule.rationale: AI will amplify and extend the foundational structures provided to it. An inconsistent or poorly structured system fed into AI tooling produces inconsistency at scale — faster and at higher volume than manual processes.

rule.statement: Do not use AI design system tooling for a product with fewer than approximately 30 components. The automation overhead (pipeline setup, review gates, tool maintenance) exceeds the manual maintenance cost at small scale.
rule.rationale: Automation has a fixed setup and maintenance cost. For small component libraries, manual maintenance is faster and more accurate than configuring and reviewing AI-generated outputs.

rule.statement: Run AI documentation generation on a schedule (weekly CI job), not on every commit. Commit-level doc generation creates noise and increases review burden without proportional value.
rule.rationale: Component APIs change infrequently compared to commit frequency. Weekly regeneration captures meaningful changes; per-commit generation produces mostly identical diffs.

rule.statement: Include "do not use" examples in every AI-generated component doc. Positive examples alone are insufficient — agents default to showing only the happy path.
rule.rationale: Developers need to know misuse patterns, not just correct usage. Docs without antipatterns lead to prop misuse and accessibility violations.

section.title: Token Enforcement Rules

rule.statement: Gate AI-generated component variants behind a design review milestone before merging to the system. Never auto-merge variant additions without human designer review.
rule.rationale: AI-generated variants often violate implicit design rules not captured in tokens (e.g., "buttons never have rounded corners in data-dense views"). Token compliance alone is insufficient for variant correctness.

rule.statement: Diff Figma component properties against code prop interfaces monthly. Timestamp both sources when diffing — Figma API data may lag code by weeks.
rule.rationale: Design-to-code drift accumulates silently. Monthly diffs catch misalignment before it becomes a significant inconsistency problem requiring a migration.

section.reference: Scan CSS and component files for hardcoded color and spacing values

antipattern.description: Connecting AI tooling to a design system with no agreed single source of truth for tokens
antipattern.reason: AI will silently pick one source and ignore the other. The result is a system where Figma and code drift further apart, faster.

antipattern.description: Auto-merging AI-generated Storybook stories without reviewing edge cases and error states
antipattern.reason: AI-generated stories default to the happy path. Error states, disabled states, and loading states are systematically missing unless explicitly prompted.
